name: lemma_entry_sizes_aligned_external_body
reason: `lemma_entry_sizes_aligned` is `external_body` — the alignment relationship between layer entry sizes is trusted without proof

name: self_aligned
reason: Every entry size is self-aligned — trivially true for any nonzero nat, but derived from the external_body axiom rather than from first principles

name: aligned_auto_instantiation
reason: Layer 0's entry size is aligned to layer 1's — follows from auto lemma which itself depends on the unverified external_body base lemma

name: adjacent_layers_divisible
reason: Adjacent layer entry sizes are divisible — the `entry_size_is_next_layer_size` invariant makes `entry_size(i) == entry_size(i+1) * num_entries(i+1)`, so divisibility is a verified consequence, but the alignment lemma's external_body makes even this path unverified

name: aligned_transitive_via_external
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_transitive_via_external(arch: Arch, i: nat, j: nat, k: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i <= j,
        j <= k,
        k < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        aligned(arch.entry_size(i), arch.entry_size(k)),
{
    arch.lemma_entry_sizes_aligned(i, k);
}
reason: Alignment is transitive across non-adjacent layers — this skips intermediate layers entirely, relying solely on the external_body axiom without verifying the inductive chain
